title: Optimizing React Memoized Component Re-renders Due to Changing State Array

statement.markdown: A React function component, `TodoList`, is designed to render a list of todo items. It uses the `useState` hook to manage its state, which is an array of todo items. The component is memoized with the `useMemo` hook to prevent unnecessary re-renders. However, when the state array is modified by adding or removing an item, the component re-renders unnecessarily due to the shallow equality check of the `useMemo` hook. Fix the bug to optimize the component re-renders.
statement.inputFormat: A JSON object representing the todo list state
statement.outputFormat: The rendered HTML string of the todo list
statement.notes: The component should re-render when the todo list state changes, but not unnecessarily due to shallow equality checks.

constraints.description: The number of todo items in the list
The number of times the todo list is modified

examples.explanation: The initial todo list state with 2 items
The todo list state after modifying the first item

hints.text: Understand the `useMemo` hook and shallow equality checks

editorial.title: Optimizing React Memoized Component Re-renders
editorial.intuition: The `useMemo` hook is used to memoize the component, but it has a shallow equality check. This means that when the state array is modified, the component re-renders unnecessarily.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible solution is to use a deep equality check, but this can be inefficient. A better solution is to use the `useReducer` hook to manage the state and avoid shallow equality checks.
editorial.betterSolution: Use the `useReducer` hook to manage the state and avoid shallow equality checks.
editorial.optimalSolution: Implement the `useReducer` hook with a deep equality check to prevent unnecessary re-renders.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The `useReducer` hook ensures that the component re-renders only when the state changes, not unnecessarily due to shallow equality checks.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run of the corrected component
